steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined phrase 'sprouted from the ground like ancient trees in a petrified forest.' This is a simile embedded in a description of tall, thin columns still standing among ruins. The task is to match what this figurative language actually does in context.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It presents an image to illustrate what one part of the ruins looks like': the simile compares columns to ancient trees to convey their height, spindly form, and weathered persistence — a visual illustration of the ruins. ✓
  • Choice B — 'It emphasizes that the narrator already knows a lot about the city': the simile shows Darius perceiving what he sees, not displaying prior knowledge about Persepolis. ✗
  • Choice C — 'It suggests that the narrator had expected to visit a forest': nothing in the text hints at a prior expectation of a forest; the comparison is descriptive, not about expectations. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It indicates the specific time in the past when the buildings became ruins': the phrase gives no historical dating; 'petrified' here is figurative (still, ancient-looking), not a geological timestamp. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
